title: release/18.x: [InstCombine] Fix infinite loop in select equivalence fold (#84036)
summary: The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that `select` with an equality condition can propagate value equivalence into its true/false arms. The original case handled `select (icmp eq A, B), A, f(A)` → `select ..., B, f(A)` when `f` is a GEP. The patch extends this to any instruction where replacing `A` with `B` (or vice versa) simplifies the arm, using `simplifyWithOpReplaced`. To prevent infinite loops, they require either the replacement value or the simplified result to be constant—a conservative but effective guard. This now covers diverse cases like arithmetic identities (`sub A, B` when `A == B` → `0`), reversible operations (bitreverse), and logical reductions. The key insight: decouple the transformation from specific opcodes and instead rely on general simplification after value substitution, with a loop-safety check.
